title: Maverick Trading Rules Explained: Drawdown, Targets & Payouts (2026)

excerpt: Maverick Trading rules explained: drawdown, targets, payouts, and costs for 2026. See how it compares to other prop firms and if it's right for you.
content:
Table of Contents

  • What is Maverick Trading?
  • Maverick Trading Rules Explained (2026)
  • Drawdown Rules and Risk Management
  • Profit Targets and Performance Metrics
  • Payout Structure and Profit Splits
  • Quick Comparison: Maverick vs Other Prop Firms
  • Pros and Cons of Maverick Trading
  • Is Maverick Trading Worth It in 2026? Our Recommendation

What is Maverick Trading?

Maverick Trading is one of the oldest and most established proprietary trading firms in the United States, with a 25+ year track record. Unlike many newer prop firms that focus on rapid funding through trading challenges, Maverick takes a training-first approach. Their program is designed for traders who want to build real skills in US equities and options, with a strong emphasis on risk management, mentorship, and long-term profitability. For a detailed breakdown of their platform, see our Maverick Trading review.

Maverick Trading Rules Explained (2026)

If you’re considering joining Maverick Trading in 2026, it’s crucial to understand their rules. Unlike challenge-based firms, Maverick’s rules are built around professional risk controls, ongoing performance metrics, and a structured payout system. Here’s what you need to know:

  • Drawdown limits are strictly enforced to protect both trader and firm capital.
  • Profit targets are set to encourage consistent, risk-adjusted returns.
  • Payouts are based on a tiered profit split, with up to 80% going to the trader.
  • Program cost is higher than most challenge-based firms: $2,500/month as of 2026.
  • Markets traded: US equities and options only (no forex, futures, or crypto).

Let’s break down each of these rules in detail.

Drawdown Rules and Risk Management

Maverick Trading is known for its strict risk management protocols. Here’s how their drawdown rules work in 2026:

  • Maximum Drawdown: Typically set at 5-10% of your allocated capital. For example, if you’re trading with $100,000 in firm capital, your max drawdown is $5,000–$10,000.
  • Daily Loss Limits: You cannot lose more than a set percentage (often 2%) of your capital in a single day.
  • Position Sizing: Strict guidelines on position size and leverage, especially for options trades.
  • Mandatory Stop-Losses: Every trade must have a stop-loss order in place.

Violating these rules can result in reduced capital allocation or removal from the program. This is a key difference from challenge-based firms, which often allow more flexibility but less support. For a side-by-side look at how Maverick’s risk controls compare to other firms, check our comparison page.

Profit Targets and Performance Metrics

Maverick Trading’s profit targets are designed to reward consistency, not just big wins. Here’s what you’ll be measured on:

  • Monthly Profit Targets: Typically 2-5% of allocated capital per month. For a $100,000 account, that’s $2,000–$5,000 in net profits.
  • Risk-Adjusted Returns: Your Sharpe ratio and win/loss consistency are tracked. Maverick values traders who can deliver steady returns with controlled risk.
  • Minimum Trading Days: You must trade a minimum number of days per month (often 10-15) to remain active and eligible for payouts.
  • Strategy Adherence: Traders are expected to stick to their approved trading plan and risk parameters.

Unlike some prop firms that only care about passing a one-time challenge, Maverick’s ongoing evaluation ensures you’re building sustainable trading habits. For more on how this compares to other firms, see our comparison page.

Payout Structure and Profit Splits

Maverick Trading offers a competitive payout structure, especially for traders who demonstrate consistency and risk management:

  • Profit Split: Up to 80% of net trading profits go to the trader. The exact split depends on your tenure and performance.
  • Payout Frequency: Monthly, with no minimum withdrawal threshold.
  • Scaling Up: Consistent traders can request increased capital allocation, which boosts potential earnings.
  • Program Cost: $2,500/month (2026), which covers training, mentorship, and access to firm capital.

While the upfront cost is higher than most challenge-based firms, the potential for long-term earnings and skill development is significant. For a detailed breakdown, see our Maverick Trading review.

Quick Comparison: Maverick vs Other Prop Firms

Prop Firm Rating Monthly Cost Profit Split Markets Best For
Maverick Trading 3.9/5 $2,500 Up to 80% US equities, options Beginner traders, options, education-focused
Apex Trader Funding 4.5/5 $147 Up to 90% Futures Futures traders, low-cost challenge
AquaFunded 4.2/5 $99 Up to 85% Forex, indices, crypto Forex/CFD traders, fast funding
Blue Guardian 4.0/5 $89 Up to 85% Forex, indices, crypto Low-cost, challenge-based

For more detailed comparisons, check out our comparison pages.

Pros and Cons of Maverick Trading

  • Pros:
    • One of the most established US prop firms (25+ years)
    • Training-first model with structured mentorship
    • Up to 80% profit split for funded traders
    • Focus on real skill-building, not just passing a challenge
    • Strong community and ongoing support
  • Cons:
    • High upfront program cost ($2,500/month in 2026)
    • Limited to US equities and options (no forex, futures, or crypto)
    • Not suitable for traders seeking instant funding or low-cost entry

For a full breakdown of user experiences, see our Maverick Trading review.

Is Maverick Trading Worth It in 2026? Our Recommendation

If you’re a beginner or intermediate trader looking for a structured, mentorship-driven path to professional trading, Maverick Trading is one of the best options in 2026. The higher upfront cost is justified by their deep educational resources, strict risk management, and the potential for long-term growth. However, if you want to trade forex, futures, or crypto, or if you’re seeking a low-cost, fast-funding challenge, you’ll find better options elsewhere (see our comparison pages for alternatives).

Bottom line: Maverick Trading is best for traders who value education, mentorship, and a professional approach to risk management over quick funding or low fees. If that’s you, their rules and structure in 2026 make them a top pick for building a sustainable trading career.
